Get Started
Version History

Changelog

Every release, feature, and fix — from the first MCP server prototype to today's hybrid Rust architecture with tree-sitter AST parsing.

v1.5.1 Bug Fix March 24, 2026 Release

Fix recursive alias loop causing SIGABRT

  • Fixed critical bug where lean-ctx init --global caused git commands to hang and crash on fish shell and some Linux configurations
  • Added LEAN_CTX_ACTIVE env var guard to prevent infinite recursion in subprocess spawning
  • CLI commands no longer spin up a tokio multi-threaded runtime — only MCP server and dashboard use async
  • Shell aliases from init --global are now wrapped in a guard condition for fish, bash, and zsh

Fish shell always loads config.fish even for non-interactive shells, causing the aliases to re-trigger lean-ctx in an infinite loop. The fix works at the binary level (passthrough mode) so even existing unguarded alias installations are protected.

v1.5.0 Major Feature March 24, 2026 Release

Tree-sitter AST-based Signature Extraction

  • Integrated tree-sitter for AST-powered code analysis across 10 languages
  • Supports Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Java, C, C++, Ruby
  • Correctly handles multi-line signatures, arrow functions, nested classes, and visibility modifiers
  • Regex-based extraction remains as automatic fallback for unsupported languages (Svelte, Vue, etc.)
  • Optional Cargo feature flag — build without tree-sitter using --no-default-features for a ~5.7 MB binary

The previous regex-based signature extraction was brittle for complex code patterns. Tree-sitter provides a proper Abstract Syntax Tree, enabling accurate extraction of function signatures, class definitions, impl blocks, type aliases, and more. Each language uses hand-crafted SCM queries optimized for the structures developers care about.

v1.4.1 Patch March 24, 2026 Release

Dashboard Branding, Pattern Router & Metrics Accuracy

  • Fixed dashboard branding (was still showing old project name in some places)
  • Pattern router now correctly dispatches all 50+ compression patterns
  • Metrics accuracy improved — ctx_metrics tool now reflects actual session data
  • Website documentation audit and cross-link fixes
v1.4.0 Major Feature March 24, 2026

50+ Compression Patterns, New CLI Commands & Homebrew

  • 50+ shell compression patterns covering git, docker, npm/pnpm/yarn, cargo, kubectl, gh CLI, pip, linters, build tools, test runners, and more
  • New CLI commands: discover (find uncompressed commands in history), session (adoption stats), config (configuration management)
  • Homebrew support: brew install yvgude/lean-ctx/lean-ctx
  • Full comparison table vs. RTK in README and website
  • Discord community launch

This release transformed lean-ctx from a pure MCP server into a hybrid architecture with transparent CLI compression. Any shell command output can be piped through lean-ctx for automatic token reduction.

v1.3.2 Docs March 24, 2026

Complete Documentation & leanctx.com Launch

  • Comprehensive documentation for all 8 MCP tools with examples
  • Token Dense Dialect (TDD) section added to crates.io README
  • All links updated to leanctx.com
v1.3.1 Enhancement March 24, 2026

TDD in All 8 MCP Tools

  • Token Dense Dialect output mode now available in all 8 MCP tools
  • Consistent symbol shorthand across ctx_read, ctx_tree, ctx_shell, ctx_search, ctx_compress, ctx_analyze, ctx_benchmark, ctx_metrics
v1.3.0 Major Feature March 24, 2026

Token Dense Dialect (TDD)

  • Introduced Token Dense Dialect — a compressed output format using symbol shorthand
  • Symbol table mapping (§MAP) for compact identifier references
  • Uses symbols like → ∴ ≈ ✓ ✗ λ ∂ § to maximize information density
  • Configurable via MCP instructions (CRP MODE: tdd)

TDD is a response formatting protocol that tells lean-ctx to output in the most token-efficient form possible. Articles, filler words, and pleasantries are stripped. Identifiers are mapped to short IDs. Tabular format is used for structured data.

v1.2.2 Enhancement March 23, 2026

Auto-Checkpoint & Mandatory MCP Instructions

  • Auto-checkpoint every 10 tool calls — compresses session context automatically
  • MCP server instructions are now mandatory and injected into every session
  • Full tool call tracking across all 8 MCP tools

The auto-checkpoint feature creates compressed summaries of session state at regular intervals, preventing context windows from growing unboundedly in long coding sessions.

v1.2.1 Enhancement March 23, 2026

Dashboard & Full MCP Tracking

  • Restored web dashboard command (lean-ctx dashboard)
  • All 8 MCP tools now tracked in persistent statistics
  • Dashboard shows per-tool savings breakdown
v1.2.0 Major Feature March 23, 2026

Web Dashboard & Persistent MCP Stats

  • Web dashboard at localhost:3333 for real-time token savings visualization
  • Persistent statistics stored in ~/.lean-ctx/stats.json
  • Simplified MCP server instructions for better LLM compliance
  • Daily aggregates and per-command breakdowns

The dashboard provides a visual overview of cumulative token savings, most-compressed commands, daily usage patterns, and adoption metrics.

v1.1.1 Patch March 23, 2026

Fix GitHub Repository URL & MCP Version

  • Corrected GitHub repository URL on crates.io metadata
  • Fixed MCP server version string
  • Fixed grep quoting issue in CLI
v1.1.0 Major Feature March 23, 2026

Full CLI Parity & Public Release

  • Published on crates.io and GitHub
  • Full CLI command suite: gain, init, read, diff, grep, find, ls, deps
  • lean-ctx init --global for one-command shell integration
  • Updated website with CLI documentation

This release marked the transition from an internal tool to a publicly available package. All install instructions were updated for cargo and source builds.

v1.0.0 Major Release March 23, 2026

Complete Rust Rewrite — Hybrid Architecture

  • Full rewrite in Rust — single binary, no Node.js runtime needed
  • Hybrid architecture: MCP server + shell hook in one binary
  • Dependency map mode with structured headers
  • Session-aware caching with file references (F1, F2, ...)
  • Compact Response Protocol (CRP) v2

The Rust rewrite eliminated the need for a separate Node.js runtime, reduced startup time to near-zero, and enabled the hybrid shell hook architecture that intercepts and compresses CLI output transparently.

v0.5.0 Feature March 23, 2026

Entropy Compression & ctx_analyze

  • Shannon entropy filtering for intelligent content reduction
  • Jaccard deduplication to collapse similar code blocks
  • ctx_analyze tool for optimal read mode recommendation
v0.4.0 Feature March 23, 2026

ctx_compress Tool

  • New ctx_compress MCP tool for on-demand text compression
  • Token cost guide documentation
  • Optimized Cursor rule for lean-ctx integration
v0.3.0 Feature March 23, 2026

Dashboard, CLI & Persistence

  • First version of the web dashboard
  • CLI interface for direct usage
  • Persistent statistics across sessions
  • GitHub Copilot support
v0.2.0 Feature March 23, 2026

Token Counter, Compact Protocol & Benchmarks

  • tiktoken-based accurate token counting
  • Compact Response Protocol (CRP) for structured output
  • ctx_benchmark tool for comparing compression modes
v0.1.0 Initial Release March 23, 2026

lean-ctx MCP Server v0.1.0

  • First release of the lean-ctx MCP Server
  • Core ctx_read tool with caching and signature extraction
  • ctx_search and ctx_tree tools
  • Basic compression: diff-updates, signature-only mode, syntax stripping

The initial concept: an MCP server that sits between the IDE and the LLM, compressing file contents and tool outputs to reduce token consumption while preserving the information the model actually needs.

Want to see what's coming next?

MIT · Rust · Open Source

Hybrid Context Optimizer.
89–99% fewer tokens for AI coding tools.
